metadata.name: GRC Framework Ontology (Governance, Risk & Compliance)
metadata.description: Governance-centric hub ontology for the GRC series. Governance is the superordinate domain — risk, compliance, security, AI governance, and resource economics operate as governed domains within it. Aligned to COBIT 2019 (EDM01-05), ISO 38500:2024, NIST CSF 2.0, IIA Three Lines Model 2020, and the NCSC Cyber Governance Code 2025. Provides 15 owned entities covering governance frameworks, bodies, decision rights, policies, assurance activities, findings, governance codes, principles, board-level actions, glossary terms, cross-framework mappings, regulatory requirements, and compliance frameworks. 4-level governance model (Platform/Instance/Product/Client). Cross-domain edges to ERM, COMP-FW, SEC-FW, AI-GOV, RES and VSEM bridges to VE-Series.
metadata.purpose: Provide the definitive governance hub for the GRC series, modelling how governance structures direct and monitor risk, compliance, security, AI governance, and resource management domains through the Evaluate-Direct-Monitor cycle, COBIT governance objectives, IIA Three Lines accountability, and cross-framework alignment
metadata.creator: Azlan EA-AAA

oaa:sourceFrameworks.name: Cyber Governance Code of Practice
oaa:sourceFrameworks.publisher: UK National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) / Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
applicability: UK board directors and senior leaders
Public sector bodies and critical national infrastructure operators

oaa:sourceFrameworks.name: COBIT 2019 — Governance System for Enterprise IT
NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
oaa:sourceFrameworks.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)

oaa:imports.name: CTX-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Context ontology providing GeoContext, FunctionalMaturity, ITMaturity, and MarketClassification dimensions for jurisdiction, maturity, and market linkage

oaa:imports.name: NCSC-CAF-ONT
oaa:imports.description: NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework ontology providing CAFPrinciple and ContributingOutcome entities for governance-to-assessment alignment

oaa:imports.name: ORG-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Organization ontology providing Organization entity for domain authority publisher references

oaa:imports.name: VSOM-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Vision & Strategic Objectives Model — VE-Series hub providing VisionStatement and StrategicObjective for governance-to-strategy alignment (VSEM bridge)

oaa:imports.name: ERM-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Enterprise Risk Management ontology — GRC Tier 1 domain providing RiskRegister and Risk for governance-to-risk bridges

oaa:imports.name: SEC-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Security Framework ontology — GRC Tier 1 domain providing SecurityPosture and SecurityDomain for governance-to-security bridges

oaa:imports.name: COMP-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Compliance Framework ontology — GRC Tier 1 domain providing ComplianceAssessment for governance-to-compliance bridges

oaa:imports.name: AI-GOV-ONT
oaa:imports.description: AI Governance ontology — GRC Tier 1 domain providing AISystem for governance-to-AI bridges

oaa:joinPatterns.name: GRC Framework to CTX Jurisdiction Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links regulatory requirements to geographic context for jurisdiction-specific compliance
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Determine which regulations apply based on organisational operating regions

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance Framework to Body to Code Hierarchy
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Navigates from governance framework through body ownership to published codes, principles and board actions
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: grc-fw:GovernanceFramework → ns:hasGovernanceBody → ns:GovernanceBody → ns:bodyOwnsCode → ns:GovernanceCode → ns:hasPrinciple → ns:GovernancePrinciple → ns:hasAction → ns:GovernanceAction
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Trace governance accountability from framework through body to specific board actions

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance Action to CAF Alignment Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance actions to NCSC CAF principles for cross-framework compliance alignment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map governance board actions to operational CAF assessment outcomes

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance Code to Authority Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance codes to their publishing domain authority organization
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Identify which domain authorities publish governance codes and their jurisdictional scope

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance Assurance to Finding Remediation
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Navigates from assurance activity through findings to affected policies for remediation tracking
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Track assurance findings through to policy remediation lifecycle

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance to Security Posture Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance context through to security posture for security-specific governance oversight
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Governance oversight of security posture and maturity across security domains

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Governance to Risk Register Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance context to enterprise risk registers for risk-informed governance decisions
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Board-level visibility of enterprise risk registers and appetite alignment

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VSEM Strategy Alignment Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance framework to VE-Series strategic objectives for strategy-governance alignment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Ensure governance framework is aligned to organisational vision and strategic direction

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VSEM Body Achievement Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links governance body to strategic objectives it is accountable for achieving
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map governance body accountability to strategic objective delivery

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VSEM Decision Alignment Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links decision rights to strategic objectives requiring governance alignment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Ensure governance decisions are grounded in strategic objectives

oaa:joinPatterns.name: VSEM Finding Impact Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Links assurance findings to the strategic objectives they impact
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Quantify strategic impact of assurance findings for board-level reporting

entities.name: GRCContext
entities.description: Top-level Governance, Risk & Compliance context defining which GRC requirements apply to an organisation. Evolved from RCSGContext — now serves as the root governance context bridging to all GRC Tier 1 domains (ERM, COMP-FW, SEC-FW, AI-GOV, RES) and downstream implementation ontologies. Supports 4-level governance scoping.

properties.name: grcProfileId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this GRC context profile

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client — determines which governance policies and controls apply

properties.name: compliancePosture
properties.description: Overall compliance posture: Non-Compliant, Partial, Substantially-Compliant, Fully-Compliant, Certified

properties.name: primaryRegulator
properties.description: Primary regulatory body overseeing the organisation (e.g., FCA, ICO, OFCOM, CQC)

properties.name: dataClassification
properties.description: Highest data classification handled: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted, Top-Secret

properties.name: auditFrequency
properties.description: Required audit frequency: Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Continuous

properties.name: sectorClassification
properties.description: Sector for regulatory scoping: CNI, Financial-Services, Healthcare, Public-Sector, Defence, General

entities.name: GovernanceFramework
entities.description: An adopted governance framework defining how the organisation evaluates, directs, and monitors IT and cyber governance. Aligned to ISO 38500:2024 Evaluate-Direct-Monitor cycle, COBIT 2019 EDM objectives, and NIST CSF 2.0 GOVERN function. References framework standards — full process models reside in PE-Series.

properties.name: frameworkId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this governance framework adoption

properties.name: frameworkName
properties.description: Name of the governance framework (e.g., COBIT 2019, ISO 38500, NIST CSF 2.0, Custom)

properties.name: frameworkType
properties.description: Framework classification: IT-Governance, Cyber-Governance, AI-Governance, Data-Governance, Integrated

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

properties.name: maturityModel
properties.description: Maturity model used: COBIT-CMMI, ISO-38500-Principles, NIST-CSF-Tiers, Custom

properties.name: maturityScore
properties.description: Current governance maturity score (0-5 CMMI scale or framework-specific)

properties.name: adoptedStandards
properties.description: Comma-separated standard references adopted under this framework (e.g., ISO-38500, COBIT-EDM01, NIST-GV)

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope of framework applicability: Enterprise, Division, Business-Unit, Programme, Project

properties.name: edmCycle
properties.description: ISO 38500 Evaluate-Direct-Monitor cycle status: Evaluate, Direct, Monitor, or All-Active

properties.name: cobitObjectives
properties.description: COBIT 2019 governance objectives adopted: comma-separated from EDM01-EDM05

properties.name: nistGovCategories
properties.description: NIST CSF 2.0 GOVERN categories adopted: GV.OC, GV.RM, GV.RR, GV.PO, GV.OV, GV.SC

properties.name: reviewDate
properties.description: Next scheduled framework review date

entities.name: GovernanceBody
entities.description: A governance body responsible for oversight, direction, or assurance within the governance framework. Aligned to IIA Three Lines Model 2020 — each body has an explicit Three Lines role (Line 1: management/ownership, Line 2: oversight/expertise, Line 3: independent assurance). Maps to COBIT 2019 EDM01 governance structures and NCSC Cyber Governance Code E1 (Establish governance structure).

properties.name: bodyId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this governance body

properties.name: bodyName
properties.description: Name of the governance body (e.g., Main Board, Risk Committee, Architecture Review Board)

properties.name: bodyType
properties.description: Body type classification: Board, SteeringCommittee, RiskCommittee, AuditCommittee, TechnologyCommittee, ARB, AIEthicsBoard, DataGovernanceBoard, SecurityForum

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

properties.name: threeLineRole
properties.description: IIA Three Lines role: Line1-Management, Line2-Oversight, Line3-Assurance, or Governing-Body

properties.name: decisionRights
properties.description: Comma-separated decision domains this body has authority over

properties.name: meetingCadence
properties.description: Meeting frequency: Weekly, Fortnightly, Monthly, Quarterly, Annual, Ad-Hoc

properties.name: charter
properties.description: Reference to the body's terms of reference or charter document

properties.name: escalationPath
properties.description: Body to escalate unresolved issues to (bodyId reference)

properties.name: reportsTo
properties.description: Parent body this body reports to (bodyId reference)

entities.name: DecisionRight
entities.description: A formalised decision authority assigned to a governance body, defining what decisions can be made, by whom, within what thresholds, and using what RACI pattern. Aligned to COBIT 2019 EDM01 (Ensured Governance Framework Setting) and ISO 38500 Direct principle.

properties.name: decisionId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this decision right

properties.name: decisionDomain
properties.description: Domain of decision authority: Investment, Architecture, Security, Risk-Acceptance, Data-Classification, Vendor-Selection, Policy-Approval, Technology-Adoption, AI-Deployment, Regulatory-Response, Benefit-Realisation

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

properties.name: raciRole
properties.description: RACI accountability for this body: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed

properties.name: thresholdValue
properties.description: Threshold above which this decision right applies (e.g., >£50k, >High-Risk, >Tier-2-AI)

properties.name: delegationRules
properties.description: Conditions under which this decision right can be delegated to a lower governance level

properties.name: approvalQuorum
properties.description: Required quorum or approval pattern: Unanimous, Majority, Chair-Approval, Delegated

entities.name: GovernancePolicy
entities.description: A governance policy, standard, guideline, procedure, baseline, or exception within the policy hierarchy. Aligned to COBIT 2019 APO01 (Managed I&T Management Framework) policy cascade and NCSC Cyber Governance Code B3 (Ensure appropriate policies and processes). Policy hierarchy: Policy → Standard → Guideline → Procedure → Baseline → Exception.

properties.name: policyId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this policy document

properties.name: policyName
properties.description: Name of the policy document

properties.name: policyType
properties.description: Position in policy hierarchy: Policy, Standard, Guideline, Procedure, Baseline, Exception

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

properties.name: enforcementLevel
properties.description: Enforcement: Mandatory, Advisory, Voluntary, Conditional

properties.name: appliesToDomain
properties.description: GRC domain this policy applies to: Governance, Risk, Compliance, Security, AI, Resources, Cross-Domain

properties.name: reviewCycle
properties.description: Review frequency: Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly, Event-Driven

properties.name: lastReviewDate
properties.description: Date of last policy review

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Policy owner role or individual reference

properties.name: parentPolicy
properties.description: Parent policy this derives from in the hierarchy (policyId reference)

properties.name: regulatoryDriver
properties.description: Regulation or standard that mandates this policy (e.g., UK-GDPR-Art25, NIS2-Art21, AI-Act-Art9)

entities.name: GovernanceAssurance
entities.description: An assurance activity providing evidence of governance effectiveness. Aligned to COBIT 2019 MEA (Monitor, Evaluate, Assess) domain, IIA Three Lines Model Line 3, and NCSC Cyber Governance Code Principle E (Assurance & Oversight). Covers internal audit, external audit, penetration testing, red team exercises, certification audits, regulatory inspections, vendor assessments, and AI model audits.

properties.name: assuranceId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this assurance activity

properties.name: assuranceName
properties.description: Name or title of the assurance activity

properties.name: assuranceType
properties.description: Type: InternalAudit, ExternalAudit, PenTest, RedTeam, CertificationAudit, RegulatoryInspection, VendorAssessment, AI-Model-Audit

properties.name: assuranceLevel
properties.description: Level of assurance provided: Limited, Reasonable, Absolute

properties.name: governanceLevel
properties.description: Governance scoping level: Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

properties.name: targetDomain
properties.description: GRC domain being assured: Governance, Risk, Compliance, Security, AI, Resources, Cross-Domain

properties.name: maturityScore
properties.description: Maturity score resulting from assurance (0-5 CMMI scale)

properties.name: conductedBy
properties.description: Assurance provider: Internal, External-Big4, External-Specialist, Regulator, Peer-Review

properties.name: conductedDate
properties.description: Date the assurance activity was conducted

properties.name: nextScheduledDate
properties.description: Next scheduled assurance activity date

properties.name: findingCount
properties.description: Number of findings raised

properties.name: overallRating
properties.description: Overall assurance rating: Satisfactory, Needs-Improvement, Unsatisfactory, Critical

properties.name: frameworkRef
properties.description: Framework or standard the assurance is conducted against (e.g., ISO-27001, NIST-CSF-2.0, NCSC-CAF-v4)

entities.name: AssuranceFinding
entities.description: A finding raised during a governance assurance activity, tracking severity, status, remediation deadline, and the affected GRC domain. Supports the COBIT MEA finding lifecycle and IIA Three Lines assurance reporting.

properties.name: findingId
properties.description: Unique identifier for this finding

properties.name: findingTitle
properties.description: Short title describing the finding

properties.name: findingDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of the finding, including context and evidence

properties.name: severity
properties.description: Finding severity: Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational

properties.name: status
properties.description: Finding status: Open, In-Remediation, Risk-Accepted, Closed, Overdue

properties.name: affectedDomain
properties.description: GRC domain affected: Governance, Risk, Compliance, Security, AI, Resources

properties.name: remediationDeadline
properties.description: Target date for remediation completion

properties.name: remediationOwner
properties.description: Individual or role responsible for remediation

properties.name: riskAcceptedBy
properties.description: If status is Risk-Accepted, the governance body or individual who accepted the risk

properties.name: regulatoryImplication
properties.description: Regulatory consequence if not remediated: None, Reportable, Penalty-Risk, Licence-Risk

entities.name: RegulatoryRequirement
entities.description: Specific regulatory requirement applicable to the organisation, including the regulation name, enforcement body, jurisdiction mapping, penalty risk level, and compliance deadline. Preserved from RCSG-FW v2.0.0 — will be enhanced when COMP-FW-ONT is implemented.

properties.name: regulationId
properties.description: Standard regulation identifier (e.g., UK-GDPR, NIS2, DORA, AI-Act, SOX, PCI-DSS)

properties.name: regulationName
properties.description: Full name of the regulation or regulatory requirement

properties.name: enforcementBody
properties.description: Regulatory body enforcing this requirement (e.g., ICO, FCA, OFCOM, CQC, EU-AI-Office)

properties.name: jurisdictions
properties.description: Jurisdictions where this regulation applies — links to GeoContext operating regions

properties.name: penaltyRisk
properties.description: Non-compliance penalty risk: Low, Moderate, High, Severe, Existential

properties.name: complianceStatus
properties.description: Current status: Not-Started, In-Progress, Partially-Compliant, Compliant, Exempt

properties.name: nextReviewDate
properties.description: Next scheduled compliance review or renewal date

properties.name: aiRelevance
properties.description: AI-specific relevance: None, Indirect, Direct, AI-Specific-Regulation

entities.name: ComplianceFramework
entities.description: Compliance or security framework applied to the organisation, covering certification status, scope, and audit cycle. Preserved from RCSG-FW v2.0.0 — will be enhanced when COMP-FW-ONT is implemented.

properties.name: frameworkId
properties.description: Framework identifier (e.g., ISO27001, SOC2-TypeII, PCI-DSS-v4, MCSB-v1, CE-Plus, NIST-CSF)

properties.name: frameworkName
properties.description: Full name of the compliance or security framework

properties.name: certificationStatus
properties.description: Certification state: Not-Pursued, In-Progress, Certified, Lapsed, Exempt

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope of certification: Full-Organisation, Division, Product, Service, Infrastructure

properties.name: certificationExpiry
properties.description: Expiry date for current certification — triggers renewal workflow

properties.name: controlCoverage
properties.description: Percentage of framework controls implemented (0-100)

entities.name: GovernanceModel
entities.description: Corporate governance structure defining board composition, committee structures, policy frameworks, and decision-making authority levels. Preserved from v2.0.0 — now linked to GovernanceFramework as the parent structure.

properties.name: governanceType
properties.description: Governance model: Owner-Managed, Board-Led, Committee-Based, Distributed, Federated, Regulated-Entity

properties.name: boardComposition
properties.description: Board structure: None, Advisory, Non-Executive, Executive, Mixed, Independent-Majority

properties.name: committeesActive
properties.description: Active governance committees (comma-separated): Audit, Risk, Remuneration, Nominations, ESG, Technology, Data

properties.name: policyFramework
properties.description: Policy management approach: Ad-Hoc, Documented, Centralised, Automated, Continuously-Reviewed

properties.name: riskAppetiteStatement
properties.description: Whether a formal risk appetite statement exists and is reviewed periodically

entities.name: GovernanceCode
entities.description: A published governance code, standard, or guide authored by a domain authority providing principles and actions for board-level cyber governance oversight. Preserved from CG-ONT merge (v2.0.0).

properties.name: codeId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the governance code

properties.name: codeName
properties.description: Full name of the governance code or standard

properties.name: publisher
properties.description: Name of the publishing authority or organisation

properties.name: publisherRef
properties.description: Reference to the publisher Organization instance in ORG-ONT

properties.name: version
properties.description: Version of the code or standard

properties.name: publicationDate
properties.description: Year or date of publication

properties.name: jurisdiction
properties.description: Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction scope

properties.name: codeType
properties.description: Type classification: CodeOfPractice, Certification, Standard, Guide, or Regulation

properties.name: status
properties.description: Current status: Active, Superseded, or Draft

properties.name: url
properties.description: Official publication URL for the governance code

properties.name: supportModel
properties.description: Description of how the code supports governance implementation

entities.name: GovernancePrinciple
entities.description: A high-level governance principle within a code defining a strategic area of board-level governance responsibility. Preserved from CG-ONT merge (v2.0.0).

properties.name: principleCode
properties.description: Single-letter principle identifier (e.g. A, B, C, D, E)

properties.name: principleName
properties.description: Full name of the governance principle

properties.name: principleDescription
properties.description: Detailed description of the principle scope and purpose

properties.name: actionCount
properties.description: Number of governance actions under this principle

properties.name: principleCategory
properties.description: Category: Strategic, Operational, or Assurance

entities.name: GovernanceAction
entities.description: A specific board-level governance action under a principle defining what directors and senior leaders must do to govern cyber security effectively. Preserved from CG-ONT merge (v2.0.0).

properties.name: actionCode
properties.description: Action identifier code (e.g. A1, B2, E4)

properties.name: actionName
properties.description: Short name summarising the action

properties.name: actionDescription
properties.description: Full description of the governance action and board responsibilities

properties.name: boardRelevance
properties.description: How the action relates to board-level oversight and accountability

properties.name: dependsOn
properties.description: Comma-separated list of action codes this action depends on

entities.name: GovernanceGlossaryTerm
entities.description: A defined term from a governance code glossary providing precise definitions for key governance concepts. Preserved from CG-ONT merge (v2.0.0).

properties.name: termId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the glossary term

properties.name: term
properties.description: The glossary term word or phrase

properties.name: definition
properties.description: Full definition of the term as published in the code glossary

properties.name: relatedActionCodes
properties.description: Comma-separated action codes where this term is referenced

entities.name: GovernanceActionMapping
entities.description: A cross-framework mapping entry linking a governance action to an equivalent or related control in an external international standard. Preserved from CG-ONT merge (v2.0.0). 150 mapping instances across 9 standards in separate instance file.

properties.name: mappingId
properties.description: Unique mapping identifier (e.g. MAP-A1-CAF-001)

properties.name: targetFramework
properties.description: Name of the target framework being mapped to

properties.name: targetFrameworkVersion
properties.description: Version of the target framework

properties.name: targetControlId
properties.description: Control or section identifier in the target framework

properties.name: targetControlName
properties.description: Human-readable name of the target control or section

properties.name: mappingStrength
properties.description: Mapping strength: Full, Partial, or Related

properties.name: mappingNotes
properties.description: Additional notes on the mapping alignment context

entities.name: CAFPrinciple
entities.description: External NCSC-CAF entity representing a principle within the Cyber Assessment Framework

entities.name: ContributingOutcome
entities.description: External NCSC-CAF entity representing a contributing outcome under a CAF principle

entities.name: Organization
entities.description: External ORG-ONT entity representing a domain authority organisation that publishes governance codes and standards

entities.name: GeoContext
entities.description: External CTX entity providing geographic and jurisdictional context for regulatory applicability

entities.name: FunctionalMaturity
entities.description: External CTX entity providing per-function maturity scores driven by GRC requirements

entities.name: MarketClassification
entities.description: External CTX entity linking regulations to specific market verticals

entities.name: ITMaturity
entities.description: External CTX entity providing IT infrastructure maturity required by compliance certifications

entities.name: RiskRegister
entities.description: Forward reference — ERM-ONT entity providing enterprise risk registers for governance oversight

entities.name: Risk
entities.description: Forward reference — ERM-ONT entity providing individual risk entries informing governance decisions

entities.name: SecurityPosture
entities.description: Forward reference — SEC-FW-ONT entity providing security posture assessment for governance-to-security oversight

entities.name: SecurityDomain
entities.description: Forward reference — SEC-FW-ONT entity providing security domain classifications (Network, Identity, Data, Application, AI-Model, AI-Pipeline, Supply-Chain, etc.)

entities.name: ComplianceAssessment
entities.description: Forward reference — COMP-FW-ONT entity providing compliance assessment results for governance oversight

entities.name: AISystem
entities.description: Forward reference — AI-GOV-ONT entity providing AI system inventory for governance-to-AI oversight

entities.name: VisionStatement
entities.description: External VSOM-ONT entity — VSEM bridge target for governance-to-vision alignment

entities.name: StrategicObjective
entities.description: External VSOM-ONT entity — VSEM bridge target for governance-to-strategy alignment

relationships.name: Has GRC Context
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceFramework to its GRC organisational context

relationships.name: Has Governance Framework
relationships.description: Links a GRCContext to one or more adopted governance frameworks

relationships.name: Has Governance Body
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceFramework to its governance bodies (boards, committees, forums)

relationships.name: Has Decision Right
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceBody to its formalised decision rights

relationships.name: Has Governance Policy
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceFramework to its policies, standards, guidelines, and procedures

relationships.name: Policy Derived From
relationships.description: Links a policy to its parent in the policy hierarchy (Policy → Standard → Guideline → Procedure → Baseline → Exception)

relationships.name: Has Assurance
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceFramework to its assurance activities

relationships.name: Has Finding
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceAssurance activity to its raised findings

relationships.name: Finding Affects Policy
relationships.description: Links an AssuranceFinding to the GovernancePolicy it identifies a gap or non-compliance in

relationships.name: Body Owns Code
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceBody to governance codes it owns or is responsible for implementing

relationships.name: Has Regulatory Requirements
relationships.description: Links a GRCContext to its applicable RegulatoryRequirements

relationships.name: Has Compliance Frameworks
relationships.description: Links a GRCContext to its applied ComplianceFrameworks

relationships.name: Has Governance Model
relationships.description: Links a GRCContext to the organisation's GovernanceModel

relationships.name: Has Governance Code
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceModel to published governance codes for board-level oversight

relationships.name: Has Principle
relationships.description: A GovernanceCode contains one or more GovernancePrinciples

relationships.name: Belongs To Code
relationships.description: A GovernancePrinciple belongs to exactly one parent GovernanceCode

relationships.name: Has Action
relationships.description: A GovernancePrinciple contains one or more GovernanceActions

relationships.name: Belongs To Principle
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction belongs to exactly one parent GovernancePrinciple

relationships.name: Depends On Action
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction may depend on one or more other actions

relationships.name: Uses Glossary Term
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction references defined glossary terms

relationships.name: Has Action Mapping
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction has cross-framework mappings to external standards

relationships.name: Belongs To Action
relationships.description: A GovernanceActionMapping belongs to exactly one parent GovernanceAction

relationships.name: Governance In Context
relationships.description: A GovernanceCode is applied within a GRC organisational context

relationships.name: Governance Requires Compliance
relationships.description: A GovernanceCode requires compliance with one or more compliance frameworks

relationships.name: Regulatory Shaped By Geo
relationships.description: Links a RegulatoryRequirement to GeoContext for jurisdiction-specific compliance

relationships.name: GRC Drives Functional Maturity
relationships.description: Links GRCContext to FunctionalMaturity, reflecting how GRC requirements drive maturity needs

relationships.name: Regulatory In Market
relationships.description: Links a RegulatoryRequirement to MarketClassification for sector-specific regulation

relationships.name: Compliance Requires IT Maturity
relationships.description: Links a ComplianceFramework to ITMaturity for minimum capability requirements

relationships.name: Regulatory Implemented By GDPR
relationships.description: Links a RegulatoryRequirement to GDPR DataProtectionPrinciples

relationships.name: Framework Implemented By MCSB
relationships.description: Links a ComplianceFramework to MCSB SecurityControls for cloud security benchmark alignment

relationships.name: Framework Implemented By PII
relationships.description: Links a ComplianceFramework to PII Governance for data protection alignment

relationships.name: Governance Aligned To CAF
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction aligns to NCSC CAF principles for operational compliance bridge

relationships.name: Governance Aligned To CAF Outcome
relationships.description: A GovernanceAction aligns to NCSC CAF contributing outcomes for detailed assessment mapping

relationships.name: Published By Authority
relationships.description: A GovernanceCode is published by exactly one domain authority organization

relationships.name: Governs Risk
relationships.description: Links GRCContext to enterprise risk registers — governance oversight of risk domain. Maps to COBIT EDM03 (Ensured Risk Optimisation) and NCSC Cyber Governance Code Principle A (Risk Management).

relationships.name: Governs Security
relationships.description: Links GRCContext to security posture assessment — governance oversight of security domain. Maps to NIST CSF 2.0 GOVERN function and NCSC Cyber Governance Code Principle B (Cyber Strategy).

relationships.name: Governs Compliance
relationships.description: Links GRCContext to compliance assessments — governance oversight of compliance domain. Maps to COBIT MEA03 (Managed Compliance).

relationships.name: Governs AI
relationships.description: Links GRCContext to AI system inventory — governance oversight of AI domain. Maps to ISO 42001 AIMS and NIST AI RMF GOVERN function.

relationships.name: Policy Applies To Security Domain
relationships.description: Links a GovernancePolicy to specific security domains it governs — enables security-specific policy tracking

relationships.name: Assurance Assesses Security Posture
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceAssurance activity to the security posture it assesses — enables security assurance tracking (PenTest, RedTeam, certification)

relationships.name: Assurance Assesses Risk
relationships.description: Links a GovernanceAssurance activity to risks it evaluates — enables risk-based assurance

relationships.name: Aligns To Vision
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — Links GovernanceFramework to VisionStatement ensuring governance is aligned to organisational vision. Answers: Is governance supporting our strategic direction?

relationships.name: Supports Strategy
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — Links GovernancePolicy to StrategicObjective ensuring policies support strategic execution

relationships.name: Constrains Execution
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — GovernancePolicy constrains how execution is performed, linking governance guardrails to operational objectives

relationships.name: Validates Alignment
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — GovernanceAssurance validates that governance remains aligned to strategic objectives

relationships.name: Achieves Objective
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — GovernanceBody achieves strategic objectives through governance activities, decision-making, and oversight responsibilities

relationships.name: Requires Alignment
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — DecisionRight requires alignment to a strategic objective, ensuring governance decisions are strategically grounded

relationships.name: Measures Impact
relationships.description: VSEM bridge — AssuranceFinding measures impact on strategic objectives, linking assurance outcomes to strategic performance

oaa:enumerations.name: GovernanceLevel
oaa:enumerations.description: 4-level governance scoping model — determines which governance policies, controls, and bodies apply at each organisational tier

hasDefinedTerm.name: Platform
hasDefinedTerm.description: Platform-wide governance applying to the entire technology estate and all instances — board-level, enterprise-wide policies

hasDefinedTerm.name: Instance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Instance-level governance applying to a specific PF-Instance deployment (e.g., PFI-AIRL, PFI-W4M) — instance-specific policies and controls

hasDefinedTerm.name: Product
hasDefinedTerm.description: Product-level governance applying to a specific product or service offering — product-specific compliance and security requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: Client
hasDefinedTerm.description: Client-level governance applying to a specific client engagement — client-specific regulatory requirements and contractual obligations

oaa:enumerations.name: BodyType
oaa:enumerations.description: Classification of governance bodies by function and authority

hasDefinedTerm.name: Board
hasDefinedTerm.description: Main board of directors — ultimate governance authority

hasDefinedTerm.name: SteeringCommittee
hasDefinedTerm.description: Programme or portfolio steering committee providing strategic direction

hasDefinedTerm.name: RiskCommittee
hasDefinedTerm.description: Dedicated risk oversight committee — typically board sub-committee

hasDefinedTerm.name: AuditCommittee
hasDefinedTerm.description: Audit and assurance oversight committee — IIA Three Lines interface

hasDefinedTerm.name: TechnologyCommittee
hasDefinedTerm.description: Technology governance committee overseeing IT and digital strategy

hasDefinedTerm.name: ARB
hasDefinedTerm.description: Architecture Review Board — technical governance and standards compliance

hasDefinedTerm.name: AIEthicsBoard
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI ethics and governance board — EU AI Act / ISO 42001 compliance

hasDefinedTerm.name: DataGovernanceBoard
hasDefinedTerm.description: Data governance board overseeing data quality, privacy, and classification

hasDefinedTerm.name: SecurityForum
hasDefinedTerm.description: Information security forum or working group — operational security governance

oaa:enumerations.name: DecisionDomain
oaa:enumerations.description: Domains of decision authority aligned to COBIT EDM governance objectives

hasDefinedTerm.name: Investment
hasDefinedTerm.description: IT/AI investment decisions — COBIT EDM02 (Ensured Benefits Delivery)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Architecture
hasDefinedTerm.description: Enterprise architecture and technology standards decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Security
hasDefinedTerm.description: Security posture, control selection, and security investment decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Risk-Acceptance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Formal risk acceptance decisions — COBIT EDM03

hasDefinedTerm.name: Data-Classification
hasDefinedTerm.description: Data classification and handling requirement decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Vendor-Selection
hasDefinedTerm.description: Vendor selection and third-party risk acceptance decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Policy-Approval
hasDefinedTerm.description: Policy creation, amendment, and retirement decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Technology-Adoption
hasDefinedTerm.description: New technology and platform adoption decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Deployment
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI system deployment and risk tier classification decisions — EU AI Act

hasDefinedTerm.name: Regulatory-Response
hasDefinedTerm.description: Regulatory change impact assessment and response decisions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Benefit-Realisation
hasDefinedTerm.description: Benefits realisation review and investment case decisions — bridges to VE-Series

oaa:enumerations.name: PolicyType
oaa:enumerations.description: Policy hierarchy classification from strategic policy through to operational procedure

hasDefinedTerm.name: Policy
hasDefinedTerm.description: High-level policy statement defining intent and direction

hasDefinedTerm.name: Standard
hasDefinedTerm.description: Mandatory standard specifying minimum requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: Guideline
hasDefinedTerm.description: Advisory guideline recommending best practices

hasDefinedTerm.name: Procedure
hasDefinedTerm.description: Step-by-step operational procedure for implementation

hasDefinedTerm.name: Baseline
hasDefinedTerm.description: Minimum security or compliance baseline configuration

hasDefinedTerm.name: Exception
hasDefinedTerm.description: Documented exception to a policy or standard with risk acceptance

oaa:enumerations.name: AssuranceType
oaa:enumerations.description: Types of governance assurance activities aligned to IIA Three Lines and COBIT MEA

hasDefinedTerm.name: InternalAudit
hasDefinedTerm.description: Internal audit — IIA Three Lines Line 3 independent assurance

hasDefinedTerm.name: ExternalAudit
hasDefinedTerm.description: External audit by independent firm (Big 4 or specialist)

hasDefinedTerm.name: PenTest
hasDefinedTerm.description: Penetration testing — technical security assurance

hasDefinedTerm.name: RedTeam
hasDefinedTerm.description: Red team exercise — adversarial security assurance

hasDefinedTerm.name: CertificationAudit
hasDefinedTerm.description: Certification audit (ISO 27001, SOC2, Cyber Essentials Plus, etc.)

hasDefinedTerm.name: RegulatoryInspection
hasDefinedTerm.description: Regulatory inspection by enforcement body (FCA, ICO, CQC, etc.)

hasDefinedTerm.name: VendorAssessment
hasDefinedTerm.description: Third-party/vendor security and compliance assessment

hasDefinedTerm.name: AI-Model-Audit
hasDefinedTerm.description: AI model audit — bias testing, fairness assessment, EU AI Act conformity

oaa:enumerations.name: ThreeLineRole
oaa:enumerations.description: IIA Three Lines Model 2020 roles for governance accountability

hasDefinedTerm.name: Governing-Body
hasDefinedTerm.description: Governing body (board) — accountability to stakeholders, sets direction

hasDefinedTerm.name: Line1-Management
hasDefinedTerm.description: First line: management — owns and manages risk, delivers services

hasDefinedTerm.name: Line2-Oversight
hasDefinedTerm.description: Second line: oversight functions — expertise, monitoring, challenge

hasDefinedTerm.name: Line3-Assurance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Third line: internal audit — independent, objective assurance

oaa:enumerations.name: COBITDomain
oaa:enumerations.description: COBIT 2019 governance and management domains for cross-reference — full process models in PE-Series

hasDefinedTerm.name: EDM
hasDefinedTerm.description: Evaluate, Direct and Monitor — 5 governance objectives (EDM01-EDM05)

hasDefinedTerm.name: APO
hasDefinedTerm.description: Align, Plan and Organise — 14 management objectives (APO01-APO14)

hasDefinedTerm.name: BAI
hasDefinedTerm.description: Build, Acquire and Implement — 11 management objectives (BAI01-BAI11)

hasDefinedTerm.name: DSS
hasDefinedTerm.description: Deliver, Service and Support — 6 management objectives (DSS01-DSS06)

hasDefinedTerm.name: MEA
hasDefinedTerm.description: Monitor, Evaluate and Assess — 4 management objectives (MEA01-MEA04)

hasDefinedTerm.name: Mandatory
hasDefinedTerm.description: Legally or contractually required — non-compliance triggers formal action

hasDefinedTerm.name: Advisory
hasDefinedTerm.description: Strongly recommended — exceptions require formal risk acceptance

hasDefinedTerm.name: Voluntary
hasDefinedTerm.description: Best practice guidance — adoption encouraged but not mandated

hasDefinedTerm.name: Conditional
hasDefinedTerm.description: Required only when specific conditions are met (e.g., data classification, sector, jurisdiction)

oaa:enumerations.name: CodeType
oaa:enumerations.description: Classification of governance code publication types

hasDefinedTerm.name: CodeOfPractice
hasDefinedTerm.description: Voluntary code of practice with recommended governance actions

hasDefinedTerm.name: Certification
hasDefinedTerm.description: Certification scheme with auditable governance requirements

hasDefinedTerm.name: Standard
hasDefinedTerm.description: Formal standard published by a recognised standards body

hasDefinedTerm.name: Guide
hasDefinedTerm.description: Best practice guide or handbook for governance practitioners

hasDefinedTerm.name: Regulation
hasDefinedTerm.description: Legally binding regulation with mandatory compliance requirements

oaa:enumerations.name: CodeStatus
oaa:enumerations.description: Lifecycle status of a governance code or standard

hasDefinedTerm.name: Active
hasDefinedTerm.description: Currently active and in force

hasDefinedTerm.name: Superseded
hasDefinedTerm.description: Replaced by a newer version

hasDefinedTerm.name: Draft
hasDefinedTerm.description: Under development and not yet published

hasDefinedTerm.name: Strategic
hasDefinedTerm.description: Principles focused on strategic direction and risk appetite

hasDefinedTerm.name: Operational
hasDefinedTerm.description: Principles focused on operational security practices and people

hasDefinedTerm.name: Assurance
hasDefinedTerm.description: Principles focused on assurance, review, and continuous improvement

oaa:enumerations.name: MappingStrength
oaa:enumerations.description: Degree of alignment between a governance action and target framework control

hasDefinedTerm.name: Full
hasDefinedTerm.description: Direct and complete mapping

hasDefinedTerm.name: Partial
hasDefinedTerm.description: Partial overlap — covers some aspects

hasDefinedTerm.name: Related
hasDefinedTerm.description: Thematic relationship without direct control mapping

businessRules.name: GRC Context Requires Coverage
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one RegulatoryRequirement or ComplianceFramework linked to define minimum GRC scope

businessRules.name: Regulatory Requirement Requires Jurisdiction
businessRules.action: THEN jurisdictions MUST be specified and SHOULD map to an existing GeoContext operatingRegions value

businessRules.name: Certified Framework Requires Expiry
businessRules.condition: IF a ComplianceFramework has certificationStatus of Certified
businessRules.action: THEN certificationExpiry MUST be specified to enable renewal tracking

businessRules.name: Governance Model Requires Type
businessRules.action: THEN governanceType MUST be specified from the standard governance model types

businessRules.name: Principle Requires Code Parent
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one belongsToCode relationship to a GovernanceCode

businessRules.name: Action Requires Principle Parent
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one belongsToPrinciple relationship to a GovernancePrinciple

businessRules.name: Mapping Requires Action Parent
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one belongsToAction relationship to a GovernanceAction

businessRules.name: Code Must Have Publisher
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one publishedByAuthority relationship to an org:Organization

businessRules.name: Mapping Strength Validation
businessRules.condition: IF a GovernanceActionMapping has a mappingStrength property
businessRules.action: THEN mappingStrength MUST be one of Full, Partial, or Related

businessRules.name: Code Type Validation
businessRules.condition: IF a GovernanceCode has a codeType property
businessRules.action: THEN codeType MUST be one of CodeOfPractice, Certification, Standard, Guide, or Regulation

businessRules.name: Governance Level Required
businessRules.condition: IF a GRCContext, GovernanceFramework, GovernanceBody, DecisionRight, GovernancePolicy, or GovernanceAssurance is created
businessRules.action: THEN governanceLevel MUST be specified as Platform, Instance, Product, or Client

businessRules.name: Governance Body Requires Three Lines Role
businessRules.action: THEN threeLineRole MUST be specified as Governing-Body, Line1-Management, Line2-Oversight, or Line3-Assurance

businessRules.name: Framework Must Have At Least One Body
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one hasGovernanceBody relationship

businessRules.name: Finding Requires Assurance Parent
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST belong to exactly one GovernanceAssurance via the hasFinding relationship

businessRules.name: Policy Hierarchy Validation
businessRules.condition: IF a GovernancePolicy has a parentPolicy reference
businessRules.action: THEN the parent MUST be at a higher level in the hierarchy (Policy > Standard > Guideline > Procedure > Baseline > Exception)

businessRules.name: Governance Level Cascade
businessRules.condition: IF a GovernancePolicy has governanceLevel of Platform
businessRules.action: THEN it applies to all Instance, Product, and Client levels unless explicitly overridden by a more specific policy

businessRules.name: Security Assurance Must Reference Security Posture
businessRules.condition: IF a GovernanceAssurance has targetDomain of Security and assuranceType of PenTest or RedTeam
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have an assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture relationship to a sec-fw:SecurityPosture

examples.supportModel: Voluntary governance code supported by 22 board-level actions across 5 principles with companion cross-framework mapping to 9 international standards

examples.principleDescription: The board should ensure that the most significant risks to the organisation, including cyber risks, are identified, assessed and managed. Board members should understand the organisation's risk exposure and its risk appetite, and be able to demonstrate this through a strategic approach to risk management.

The board should ensure that cyber resilience is embedded across the organisation through a comprehensive strategy that aligns with the organisation's broader goals. This strategy should be regularly reviewed, adapted and communicated.

The board should promote a culture of cyber resilience across the organisation, ensuring that people at all levels understand their role in maintaining security. This includes fostering cyber literacy, clear policies, and a positive security culture.

The board should ensure that the organisation has appropriate plans, processes and resources to respond to and recover from cyber incidents effectively, minimising disruption and ensuring business continuity.

The board should establish appropriate governance structures to ensure effective oversight and assurance of the organisation's cyber resilience, including regular review of policies, controls, and risk appetite.

examples.actionDescription: Boards should define the organisation's cyber risk appetite as part of overall risk management, articulating acceptable levels of cyber risk in the context of the organisation's strategy and objectives.
examples.boardRelevance: Board directly sets risk appetite and ensures alignment with organisational strategy

examples.actionName: Identify key digital processes, assets and services
examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has identified and documented its key digital processes, data and digital assets, and services that are critical to business operations.
examples.boardRelevance: Board ensures comprehensive asset identification to underpin risk decisions

examples.actionName: Assess cyber risks to key processes, assets and services
examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation regularly assesses cyber risks to its identified key digital processes, assets and services, including supply chain and third-party dependencies.
examples.boardRelevance: Board reviews risk assessment results and challenges management on risk exposure

examples.actionName: Understand and manage the threat landscape
examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has access to relevant and timely cyber threat intelligence and understands how the threat landscape affects organisational risk.
examples.boardRelevance: Board receives regular threat landscape briefings and factors threats into risk appetite

examples.actionName: Manage cyber risk across the supply chain
examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation identifies, assesses and manages cyber risks arising from its supply chain and third-party relationships.
examples.boardRelevance: Board ensures supply chain cyber risk is integrated into overall risk management

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has a documented cyber strategy that aligns with the broader business strategy, covering risk management, technology, people and processes.
examples.boardRelevance: Board approves and owns the cyber strategy aligned to business objectives

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation allocates appropriate resources (financial, human, technical) to implement the cyber strategy effectively.
examples.boardRelevance: Board approves budgets and resources for cyber security implementation

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has appropriate and proportionate cyber security policies and processes in place that are regularly reviewed and updated.
examples.boardRelevance: Board ensures policy framework exists and receives summary of policy review outcomes

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has implemented effective technical controls appropriate to its size, sector and risk profile to protect against cyber threats.
examples.boardRelevance: Board receives assurance that technical controls are proportionate and effective

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation understands and complies with all relevant cyber security and data protection regulations and legal requirements.
examples.boardRelevance: Board has oversight of regulatory compliance status and material gaps

examples.actionName: Promote a positive cyber security culture
examples.actionDescription: Boards should promote and foster a positive cyber security culture where all employees understand their role in keeping the organisation secure.
examples.boardRelevance: Board sets the tone from the top for security culture and leads by example

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation provides appropriate cyber security training and awareness programmes for all staff and that there are sufficient specialist skills available.
examples.boardRelevance: Board ensures investment in training and receives reports on workforce cyber literacy

examples.actionName: Embed cyber security in roles and responsibilities
examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure that cyber security roles and responsibilities are clearly defined, documented and understood across the organisation, including a named accountable board member.
examples.boardRelevance: Board designates a lead member for cyber governance and reviews role clarity

examples.actionDescription: Board members should develop and maintain sufficient cyber literacy to understand cyber risks, challenge management effectively and make informed governance decisions.
examples.boardRelevance: Board members undertake appropriate development to maintain cyber literacy

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has a documented and tested cyber incident response plan that covers detection, containment, eradication, recovery and communication.
examples.boardRelevance: Board approves incident response plan and understands escalation triggers

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation regularly tests and exercises its incident response and business continuity plans through tabletop exercises, simulations and live drills.
examples.boardRelevance: Board participates in exercises and reviews outcomes to improve readiness

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure clear communication protocols are established for cyber incidents, including internal escalation, external stakeholder notification and regulatory reporting obligations.
examples.boardRelevance: Board understands communication responsibilities and media/regulatory obligations

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation has processes to learn from cyber incidents and near-misses, conducting post-incident reviews and implementing improvements to reduce future risk.
examples.boardRelevance: Board receives post-incident reports and ensures lessons are embedded in strategy

examples.actionDescription: Boards should establish appropriate governance structures for cyber security oversight, including clear reporting lines, committee structures and escalation paths.
examples.boardRelevance: Board designs and owns the governance structure for cyber oversight

examples.actionDescription: Boards should gain appropriate assurance that the organisation's cyber security measures are effective, through internal and external audit, penetration testing and certification schemes.
examples.boardRelevance: Board commissions and reviews assurance activities and acts on findings

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure the organisation regularly reviews and adapts its cyber governance approach in response to changes in the threat landscape, business environment and lessons learned.
examples.boardRelevance: Board reviews cyber governance effectiveness at least annually and drives adaptation

examples.actionDescription: Boards should ensure appropriate reporting on cyber governance effectiveness to relevant internal and external stakeholders, including regulators where required.
examples.boardRelevance: Board approves reporting content and ensures transparency with stakeholders

examples.definition: Chief Information Security Officer — the senior executive responsible for the organisation's information and cyber security strategy, policy and operations
The ability of individuals, particularly board members, to understand cyber security concepts, risks and governance responsibilities sufficiently to make informed decisions
An organisation's ability to prepare for, respond to and recover from cyber attacks while continuing to operate and deliver its objectives
The protection of internet-connected systems including hardware, software and data from cyber threats through technologies, processes and governance controls
The collective values, beliefs, knowledge and behaviours across an organisation that shape how people approach and prioritise cyber security in daily operations
A documented plan that sets out the organisation's approach to managing cyber risk, aligned with business strategy and covering people, process and technology dimensions
All digital information, systems, applications and technology infrastructure that support business processes and create value for the organisation
The process of obtaining evidence and confidence that cyber security measures are effective, through audit, testing, certification and other verification activities
The policies, procedures and technical measures implemented within an organisation to manage cyber risks and protect information assets
The level and type of cyber risk that an organisation is prepared to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives, as determined by the board
An individual or role within the organisation who has accountability for the management and treatment of a specific cyber risk or set of risks
The evolving set of cyber threats, threat actors, attack techniques and vulnerabilities that could affect an organisation's security posture

crossOntologyReferences.description: GRC-FW v3.0.0 is the governance-centric hub for the GRC series. Governance is superordinate — risk, compliance, security, AI governance, and resource economics are governed domains. The hub bridges to: CTX (jurisdiction, maturity, market), NCSC-CAF (assessment alignment), ORG (domain authorities), and forward references to Tier 1 domains (ERM, COMP-FW, SEC-FW, AI-GOV, RES). VSEM bridges connect to VE-Series (VSOM, OKR, RRR) for strategy-governance alignment. Security-specific edges: governsSecurity→SecurityPosture, policyAppliesToSecurityDomain→SecurityDomain, assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture→SecurityPosture.

securityMapping.governsSecurity: GRCContext → sec-fw:SecurityPosture — governance oversight of security posture
securityMapping.nistCSF2Alignment: GovernanceFramework.nistGovCategories — NIST CSF 2.0 GOVERN function categories

lineageAlignment.ctx:GeoContext: CTX GeoContext provides jurisdiction and geographic scope for regulatory applicability
lineageAlignment.ctx:FunctionalMaturity: CTX FunctionalMaturity provides per-function maturity scores driven by GRC requirements
lineageAlignment.ctx:MarketClassification: CTX MarketClassification links regulations to specific market verticals
lineageAlignment.ctx:ITMaturity: CTX ITMaturity provides IT infrastructure maturity required by compliance certifications
lineageAlignment.gdpr:DataProtectionPrinciple: GDPR-ONT defines Article 5 data protection principles that RegulatoryRequirements implement
lineageAlignment.mcsb:SecurityControl: MCSB-ONT defines cloud security benchmark controls that ComplianceFrameworks map to
lineageAlignment.pii:ComplianceFramework: PII-ONT implements data protection governance that ComplianceFrameworks reference
lineageAlignment.ncsc-caf:CAFPrinciple: NCSC-CAF CAFPrinciple receives governance action alignment for operational compliance bridging
lineageAlignment.ncsc-caf:ContributingOutcome: NCSC-CAF ContributingOutcome provides detailed assessment outcome targets
lineageAlignment.org:Organization: ORG Organization provides domain authority publisher identity for governance codes
lineageAlignment.erm:RiskRegister: ERM-ONT provides enterprise risk registers for governance-to-risk oversight (forward reference)
lineageAlignment.sec-fw:SecurityPosture: SEC-FW-ONT provides security posture for governance-to-security oversight (forward reference)
lineageAlignment.sec-fw:SecurityDomain: SEC-FW-ONT provides security domain classification for policy targeting (forward reference)
lineageAlignment.comp-fw:ComplianceAssessment: COMP-FW-ONT provides compliance assessments for governance oversight (forward reference)
lineageAlignment.ai-gov:AISystem: AI-GOV-ONT provides AI system inventory for governance-to-AI oversight (forward reference)
lineageAlignment.vsom:VisionStatement: VSOM-ONT provides vision alignment for VSEM governance-strategy bridge
lineageAlignment.vsom:StrategicObjective: VSOM-ONT provides strategic objectives for VSEM policy-strategy bridge

changeControl.changeNotes: Major version: RCSG-FW v2.0.0 → GRC-FW v3.0.0. Namespace changed from rcsg-fw to grc-fw. RCSGContext renamed to GRCContext. 6 new entities (GovernanceFramework, GovernanceBody, DecisionRight, GovernancePolicy, GovernanceAssurance, AssuranceFinding). 8 new enumerations (GovernanceLevel, BodyType, DecisionDomain, PolicyType, AssuranceType, ThreeLineRole, COBITDomain, EnforcementLevel). 15 owned entities total. 48 relationships (24 internal + 24 cross-ontology including 7 VSEM bridges). 17 business rules. 11 join patterns. Forward references to Tier 1 domains (ERM, COMP-FW, SEC-FW, AI-GOV). 7 VSEM bridges to VE-Series (alignsToVision, supportsStrategy, constrainsExecution, validatesAlignment, achievesObjective, requiresAlignment, measuresImpact). Security-specific edges: governsSecurity, policyAppliesToSecurityDomain, assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture. All CG-ONT entities and 150 cross-framework mapping instances preserved. Epic 30 F30.1.

versionHistory.notes: RCSG→GRC morph. Namespace grc-fw. 6 new entities, 8 new enums, 24 new cross-ontology relationships (inc. 7 VSEM bridges), 7 new business rules. Governance-centric hub aligned to COBIT 2019, ISO 38500, NIST CSF 2.0, IIA Three Lines. 4-level governance model. Cross-domain edges to ERM/SEC-FW/COMP-FW/AI-GOV. 7 VSEM bridges to VE-Series. Security mapping: governsSecurity, policyAppliesToSecurityDomain, assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture. Epic 30 F30.1.

Merged CG-ONT into RCSG-FW. Added GovernanceCode, GovernancePrinciple, GovernanceAction, GovernanceGlossaryTerm, GovernanceActionMapping entities. 150 cross-framework mappings to 9 international standards. Epic 30 F30.11.
Initial release: 4 entities, 10 relationships. Extracted from CTX ontology to establish abstract RCSG framework layer.
